practices.title: Avoid eval() and Function constructor

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

practices.explanation: eval() executes arbitrary code and creates XSS and code injection vulnerabilities. Use JSON.parse() for data, or redesign to avoid dynamic evaluation entirely.

standards.title: Dynamic code evaluation (eval) MUST NOT be used with user-controlled input
Dynamic code evaluation MUST NOT process user-controlled input

Verify that the application avoids the use of eval() or other dynamic code execution features. Where there is no alternative, any user input being included must be sanitized or sandboxed before being executed.
Verify that when parsing JSON in browsers or JavaScript-based backends, JSON.parse is used to parse the JSON document. Do not use eval() to parse JSON.

practices.title: Keep functions under 50 lines
practices.explanation: Functions over 50 lines are hard to test and understand. Extract logical sub-steps into named functions.

standards.title: Cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10 per function
Functions MUST have ≤5 parameters; use an options object for more
Function cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10

practices.title: Never use 'any' — use unknown or generics
practices.bad: function process(data: any): any { return data.value; }
practices.good: function process<T extends { value: string }>(data: T): string { return data.value; }
practices.explanation: 'any' disables type checking entirely. Use 'unknown' when the type is genuinely unknown and narrow it, or use generics to preserve type information.

standards.title: Cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10 per function
Functions MUST have ≤5 parameters; use an options object for more
Function cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10

practices.title: Always handle Promise rejections
practices.explanation: Unhandled Promise rejections cause silent failures in Node.js. Always attach .catch() or use try/catch with async/await.

standards.title: Async operations MUST handle rejection/error paths explicitly
Exceptional conditions MUST be caught and handled appropriately

practices.title: Avoid sequential awaits — use Promise.all

cwe.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout

practices.bad:
const a = await fetchA();
const b = await fetchB();
practices.explanation: Sequential awaits on independent async operations waste wall-clock time. Use Promise.all() to run them concurrently.

standards.title: Sequential awaits on independent async calls MUST be parallelised
Synchronous blocking I/O MUST NOT be used in async contexts
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available

practices.title: Never store secrets in source code
practices.explanation: Hardcoded secrets are committed to version control and exposed in logs, stack traces, and bundle files. Always read from environment variables or a secrets manager.

standards.title: Credentials and secrets MUST NOT be hardcoded in source files
Credentials MUST be loaded from secure external sources, never hardcoded

practices.title: Use barrel exports deliberately — avoid re-exporting everything
practices.explanation: Wildcard barrel exports create hidden coupling between modules and slow down tree-shaking. Export only what other packages genuinely need.

standards.title: Package dependency cycles MUST be 0
Module dependency graphs MUST be acyclic

practices.title: Prefer interfaces over type aliases for object shapes
practices.bad: type User = { id: string; name: string; };
practices.good: interface User { id: string; name: string; }
practices.explanation: Interfaces produce better error messages, support declaration merging, and communicate intent more clearly for object shapes. Use type aliases for unions, intersections, and primitives.

standards.title: Wildcard imports (import *) MUST NOT be used
Internal implementation details MUST be hidden behind public interfaces

practices.title: Never use innerHTML — use textContent or DOM APIs

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

practices.explanation: innerHTML interprets HTML and enables XSS attacks. Use textContent for text or a sanitization library for HTML.

standards.title: HTML output MUST be escaped or rendered through a templating engine with auto-escaping
Output rendered in HTML MUST be escaped or use auto-escaping templates

Verify that context-aware, preferably automated - or at worst, manual - output escaping protects against reflected, stored, and DOM based XSS. ([C4](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

practices.title: Always validate and sanitize external input
practices.explanation: Unsanitized input leads to injection, XSS, and data corruption. Use a validation library (zod, joi) at API boundaries.

standards.title: All user input MUST be validated and sanitised before use
All external input MUST be validated against expected format and range

Verify that all input (HTML form fields, REST requests, URL parameters, HTTP headers, cookies, batch files, RSS feeds, etc) is validated using positive validation (allow lists). ([C5](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

Verify that structured data is strongly typed and validated against a defined schema including allowed characters, length and pattern (e.g. credit card numbers, e-mail addresses, telephone numbers, or validating that two related fields are reasonable, such as checking that suburb and zip/postcode match). ([C5](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
Verify that JSON schema validation is in place and verified before accepting input.
Verify that XSD schema validation takes place to ensure a properly formed XML document, followed by validation of each input field before any processing of that data takes place.

practices.title: Use nullish coalescing (??) instead of logical OR for defaults
practices.explanation: Logical OR treats 0, '', and false as falsy. Nullish coalescing only falls through on null/undefined, preserving valid falsy values.

standards.title: Null/undefined dereferences MUST be guarded before use
Nullable references MUST be checked before dereferencing

practices.title: Always declare return types on exported functions
practices.explanation: Explicit return types catch accidental changes, improve IntelliSense, and serve as documentation for consumers.

standards.title: All public functions MUST have corresponding test files
Edge cases (empty input, boundary values) MUST be tested explicitly
Error paths MUST be tested, not only happy paths
Type annotations MUST be used on public function signatures
Error handling behaviour MUST be documented for public APIs

practices.title: Clean up resources in try/finally or AbortController

cwe.name: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime

practices.explanation: Without finally, exceptions leak resources. Always clean up in finally blocks or use AbortController/Symbol.dispose.

standards.title: Resource handles (files, connections) MUST be closed in finally blocks or using RAII patterns
Database transactions MUST be rolled back on error
Persistent operations MUST be idempotent or use transaction guards
Resources MUST be released in finally/defer blocks

practices.title: Use lazy imports for heavy modules

cwe.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

practices.bad:
import { heavyLib } from 'heavy-lib';
// heavyLib only used in rare code path
practices.explanation: Top-level imports of large libraries increase cold-start time. Use dynamic import() for code only needed in specific paths.

standards.title: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

practices.title: Avoid synchronous fs operations in server code

cwe.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout

practices.explanation: Synchronous fs calls block the event loop and stall all concurrent requests. Use fs.promises or fs callbacks.

standards.title: Sequential awaits on independent async calls MUST be parallelised
Synchronous blocking I/O MUST NOT be used in async contexts
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available

practices.title: Memoize expensive computations in React components

cwe.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

practices.explanation: Sorting or filtering on every render wastes CPU. Use useMemo to cache derived data until dependencies change.

standards.title: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops
